source_title: Run Cancellation Causes Loss of Streamed State Not Yet Persisted as a Checkpoint
pattern: Streaming output is rendered to the user but not yet checkpointed; cancellation rolls the thread back to the last persisted checkpoint and the streamed output vanishes.
expected_guard: On cancel, disconnect, or fatal error, flush the current in-memory state to the ledger as a partial/aborted checkpoint so the thread resumes from what the user actually saw.
